text: Rally the Italian restaurant staff to revolt — there is no cheese on the pizzas (it is under the sauce) and the customers have been deceived for years
Rouse the people of Paris to storm the Bastille and tear down the monarchy once and for all
Lead the factory workers out on strike — management has cut the coffee breaks and nobody is leaving until they are reinstated
Motivate your pub quiz team after coming dead last for the third week running — tonight everything changes
Convince Napoleon's exhausted army not to retreat from Moscow — turning back now would mean all of this was for nothing
Persuade your friends to push through the camping trip despite the tent flooding, the food getting eaten by something, and nobody sleeping
Rally your crew to press on to the South Pole — supplies are running low, two men have frostbite, but you are seventy miles from history
Convince the ski group to get back on the slopes after a spectacular wipeout that was witnessed by the entire mountain
Demand justice at the all-hands meeting — someone has been stealing lunches from the communal fridge for six months and it must stop today
Argue that the Great Dairy Shortage of 1947 was deliberately engineered by the government to control the population through rationing
Convince a room of skeptics that the moon landing was filmed on a studio set and the footage contains seventeen technical impossibilities
Defend the flat earth model at an academic conference and dismantle three centuries of so-called scientific consensus
Inspire your three hundred soldiers to hold the mountain pass against an army of one million — not one of you walks away, but history will remember this forever
Motivate your team to finish the entire product launch in the next two hours — the client is already in the building
Convince yourself to make the one move that wins the world chess championship — you have three minutes on the clock and one possible path to victory
Demand the burger restaurant remake your entire order because the pickles are under the lettuce instead of on top and this is not what was advertised
Argue passionately at a philosophy symposium that a hot dog is definitively not a sandwich and anyone who believes otherwise is intellectually dangerous
Argue that the book club should be dissolved and rebuilt from scratch because the same three people have been choosing every book for four years
Argue to the board that the new open-plan office is directly responsible for a forty percent drop in output and must be reversed immediately
Demand the facilities manager turn up the thermostat — it is nine degrees in the building, everyone is wearing coats at their desks, and no work is being done
Address the nation — the enemy is at the gates, every option looks impossible, but you will not yield and neither will the people behind you
Deliver the halftime team talk to a football squad losing four nil — you have forty-five minutes to reverse something that looks irreversible
Rally yourself in front of the audience after misspelling the word that knocked you out of the regional spelling bee — you will be back next year
Address the homeowners association — someone has parked in your allocated spot every single morning for three weeks and the association has done nothing
Present your case to the council that your neighbor's hedge has encroached fourteen inches onto your property and the law is on your side
Consecrate a battlefield cemetery and remind the living what the fallen were fighting for — the idea that all people are created equal
Argue that the group must watch your film choice — you have been outvoted for eleven consecutive movie nights and democracy has clearly failed
Convince your terrified friend to get on the rollercoaster — you have been queuing for two hours and the car is right there
Justify to your fitness group why today is an emergency cheat day — the logic is airtight, the reasoning is sound, and the biscuits were already open
Address the entire office in a state of declared emergency — the internet has been down for four hours, IT has not responded, and the business is collapsing
Demand the council officially designate one half of the park as a large-dog-free zone — someone's labrador knocked over four small dogs yesterday
Campaign speech promising sweeping reforms if elected class president — the vending machine situation alone is grounds for revolution
Defend your very complicated coffee order to a visibly furious barista — every modification exists for a legitimate medical or philosophical reason
Convince yourself to finish the last two minutes on the treadmill — your legs gave up four minutes ago but the person next to you is still going
Address your passengers with a plan — you have a flat tyre on a motorway at midnight, no signal, and the spare is probably in the boot under everything
Accuse your friend of cheating at Monopoly in front of the entire group — you have been watching them and the evidence is overwhelming
Deliver the best man speech having completely forgotten every word you prepared — you are standing at the microphone right now
Make the case to the queue forming behind you that using the self-checkout with a completely full trolley is your legal and moral right
